action: Cycles 339-341 (driver-agnostic routing campaign, advisory+llm) all shipped to main CI-RED because the autonomous cycle audit is cycle-scoped: it runs the current cycle's ACS predicates + scoped regression, but NOT (1) `gofmt -l` over the generated go/acs/cycle<N>/predicates_test.go files, nor (2) cross-cutting full-suite tests like cmd/evolve TestSkills_NoDrift. Consequences observed: (a) generated go/acs/cycle341/predicates_test.go had a gofmt diff (string-concat operator spacing) that failed the CI `go` workflow vet+fmt step; (b) the cycle's edits to ~70 .evolve/profiles/*.json (model_tier_default opus/sonnet -> capability tiers deep/balanced) drifted the projected phase-facts in skills/{intent,plan-review,retro,ship}/SKILL.md without regeneration, failing TestSkills_NoDrift. Fixed forward interactively in commit 965009c9 (gofmt -w + `evolve skills generate`). LOOP FIX: (1) add a gofmt-clean assertion over the cycle's generated go/acs files to the build or audit phase; (2) when a cycle edits .evolve/profiles/*.json or any SKILL.md SSOT, run `evolve skills generate` WITHIN the cycle and include the regenerated SKILL.md in the cycle commit (OR add TestSkills_NoDrift to the cycle regression scope). ROOT CAUSE: a cycle-scoped audit structurally cannot catch cross-cutting / full-suite regressions, so cycles can ship green-locally but red-in-CI. The autonomous cycle-ship path also does not watch CI (unlike the interactive /commit path), so the red ships went undetected until an operator checked.
evidence_pointer: commit 965009c9 (fix); .github/workflows/go.yml vet+fmt + coverage steps; go/cmd/evolve/cmd_skills_drift_test.go TestSkills_NoDrift
